title: trichocereus chiloensis subsp. litoralis
intro: Trichocereus chiloensis subsp. litoralis is a plant species, also known as coastal easter cactus, which is a type of cactus native to the coastal areas of Chile. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Trichocereus chiloensis subsp. litoralis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Trichocereus chiloensis subsp. litoralis is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
